# ADR-0003: Provider.call_selector protocol extension and budget-driven hook-module extraction

**Status**: accepted (auto-generated by `auto-memory-overhaul` review on 2026-05-24)
**Author**: `auto-memory-overhaul` review session
**Related initiative**: [`initiatives/_archive/2026-05-auto-memory-overhaul/`](../../initiatives/_archive/2026-05-auto-memory-overhaul/)

## Context

`auto-memory-overhaul` added LLM-based sideQuery recall and an automatic
post-turn extraction subloop to the replica. Two structural pressures
shaped the implementation. First, memory selection needs a *cheap* model
call with structured JSON output, semantically distinct from the main
agent `Provider.call()` path. Second, `loop.py` was already near the
project's hard 800-line ceiling, yet both the extraction stop-hook (M5)
and the sideQuery recall orchestration (M7) needed to run inside
`AgentLoop.run()` / `run_stream()`.

## Decision

1. **Extend the `Provider` Protocol with `call_selector(*, system, user,
   output_schema, max_tokens=256) -> dict`** rather than overloading the
   existing `call()`. `MockProvider` gains `selector_responses=[...]`
   (scripted, deterministic, raises `SelectorError` when exhausted) and
   `OpenAIProvider` gains a configurable `selector_model` (default
   `gpt-4o-mini`, JSON mode, temperature=0, capped `max_tokens`).
   Failures raise the dedicated `SelectorError`, which callers MUST catch
   and fall back to the lexical Jaccard `MemorySelector` — `SelectorError`
   never propagates out of `AgentLoop`.
2. **Extract per-turn hook logic into dedicated modules instead of
   inlining into `loop.py`.** `extraction_hooks.py` (M5) holds
   `maybe_extract_memories` + `hasMemoryWritesSince`; `recall_hooks.py`
   (M7) holds `inject_memory_attachments`. `loop.py` retains only thin
   call sites and ends at exactly 800 lines.

## Consequences

- Cheap-model selection is swappable per endpoint (DashScope / qwen /
  other OpenAI-compatible providers) without touching the main provider
  call path.
- The Jaccard selector survives as a guaranteed fallback, so recall never
  hard-fails on a selector or JSON error.
- `loop.py` stays within the size budget, and the hook modules are
  independently unit-testable; the M5 `extraction_hooks.py` pattern is
  reused verbatim by M7 `recall_hooks.py`.
- Trade-off: behavior is now split across `loop.py` + two hook modules +
  `memdir.py`, so the end-to-end turn path must be read across files.
  This split also helped several inert-wiring defects pass green unit
  tests (recent_tools always empty, read_file_state never populated,
  `fallback_used` trace hardcoded, `extraction_in_progress` gate inert) —
  see the related REVIEW.md detail findings. (All four were fixed on the
  `fix/auto-memory-inert-wiring` follow-up; the unit suite now asserts the
  integrated turn path, not just the pure functions.)
- `provider.py` grew to 867 lines adding `call_selector`, itself
  breaching the same 800-line limit the hook extraction was protecting in
  `loop.py`; a follow-up should extract the selector/JSON logic into a
  dedicated module.

## Alternatives considered

- **Overload `Provider.call()` for selection** — rejected: selection
  needs JSON mode, a cheaper model, and a small token cap; conflating it
  with the main agent call would complicate every provider implementation.
- **Inline the hooks in `loop.py`** — rejected: would push `loop.py` past
  the 800-line hard limit and make the hook logic harder to unit-test.

## Source

This ADR was auto-generated because **HANDOFF Section 2 collectively
records ≥2 design divergences, including a `Provider` Protocol change
(M6 `call_selector`) and two new-module extractions (M5
`extraction_hooks.py`, M7 `recall_hooks.py`) driven by the `loop.py`
≤800-line budget**. The full divergence record is in
`initiatives/_archive/2026-05-auto-memory-overhaul/HANDOFF.md` Section 2's
per-milestone "design decisions (deviations from PLAN)" subsections,
items: M5 (`extraction_hooks.py` extracted from loop.py), M7
(`recall_hooks.py` extracted instead of inlining), M6 (`call_selector`
Protocol addition).

Code reference (changes that this ADR explains):
```
git log --follow 6aed9ec..HEAD -- src/simple_coding_agent/loop.py src/simple_coding_agent/extraction_hooks.py src/simple_coding_agent/recall_hooks.py src/simple_coding_agent/provider.py
```

## Future updates

If a later initiative supersedes this decision:

- **Creating the new ADR is Tier B** (auto-applied by the review session
  when the supersedes trigger fires): write
  `ADR-0004-<reversal-slug>.md` with `**Status**: supersedes ADR-0003`
  and link back to this one.
- **Editing this file's Status line to `superseded by ADR-0004` is Tier
  C** (propose-only — review session writes the suggested diff in
  REVIEW.md "Proposed edits"; a human applies it after confirming the
  supersedes reasoning).

This split avoids the review session silently rewriting historical ADR
status lines without explicit human review.
